world.title: How Creditors Replaced Colonial Rule
world.description: Michael Hudson, Anne Pettifor and Françoise Vergès trace how debt replaced colonial rule, from Haiti’s forced repayments to today’s dollar-denominated crises. As war, rising energy costs, and austerity deepen global imbalances, countries face a stark choice between creditor control or economic sovereignty.

world.title: Iran offers conditional access to Hormuz, says US talks on April 10 in Islamabad
world.description: Iran said US talks will begin April 10 in Islamabad, offering to halt attacks and allow limited Strait of Hormuz access for two weeks if strikes against it stop.

business.title: T-Bill yields drop amid N6tn liquidity surge
business.description: Nigerian Treasury Bill yields fell last week as a N6tn liquidity surge boosted investor demand, causing the average benchmark yield to decline to 17.61%.

With currencies like the rupee and won at historic lows, foreign exchange interventions are necessary. The ongoing strain from elevated oil prices could prolong this economic turmoil.

science.title: 'Screams of delight': Artemis crew flying home to thrilled NASA scientists
science.description: Of note was their stunning shot of an Earthset, the moment when Earth drops below the rugged lunar horizon.

science.title: Light-driven method enables sustainable production of porous semiconducting polymers
science.description: Researchers at Koç University have developed a light-driven method to produce porous semiconducting polymers under ambient conditions without the need for metal catalysts. The study, led by Prof. Dr. Önder Metin from the Department of Chemistry, in collaboration with Dr. Melek Sermin Özer, Dr. Zafer Eroğlu, and Prof. Dr. Sermet Koyuncu, was published in Nature Communications.

science.title: 'Oldest octopus' fossil is no octopus at all, scans reveal
science.description: A famous 300-million-year-old fossil that was thought to be the world's oldest octopus—even featuring in the Guinness Book of Records—has turned out to be something else altogether. In what amounts to a case of mistaken identity, the fossil hid its true nature through decay 300 million years ago, before being fossilized.

science.title: World's Oldest Known 'Octopus' Turns Out to Be An Entirely Different Animal
science.description: A fossil that was fundamental to describing the evolution of octopuses turns out to have been an impostor, all along.

science.title: Artemis II: Conversation between Prime Minister Carney and astronaut Jeremy Hansen
science.description: On Wednesday, April 8, Canadian Space Agency (CSA) astronaut Jeremy Hansen, crewmember of the Artemis II mission around the Moon, will speak with the Prime Minister of Canada during the third and final live space-to-Earth connection. Please note that the Prime Minister will be participating in this event virtually. The Honourable Mélanie Joly, Minister of Industry and Minister responsible for Canada Economic Development for Quebec Regions will also take part in this event.

science.title: Moon’s Ice Took More Than 1.5 Billion Years to Form, New Research Suggests
science.description: A new study finds ice at the moon’s poles built up over at least 1.5 billion years, offering clues for lunar water exploration.

science.title: So long, sucker: world’s ‘oldest octopus’ is stripped of its title
science.description: The discovery of a previously hidden set of teeth in a 300-million-year-old fossil revealed it is not an octopus at all, suggesting the creatures evolved far later
